data.title: Payden & Rygel's 2026 bond market outlook highlights a divided global economy, titled "2026: A Tale of Two Cities."
data.description: Payden & Rygel have recently published their 2026 unconstrained bond market forecast titled “2026: A Tale of Two Cities – Investing Across a Bifurcated Global Economy.”

data.title: Boeing secures U.S. Air Force and Air India contracts, with a $262 million award for eight helicopters.
data.description: Shares of Boeing Company (NYSE: BA) are declining as the company revealed it had secured contracts with the U.S. Air Force and Air India airlines. Regarding the U.S. Air Force contract, Boeing will be producing four additional MH-139A Grey Wolf helicopters and providing sustainment support. This is the second production agreement for these helicopters in five months. The total value of the contract is over $262 million, with a previous award of $173 million for eight helicopters in September. The latest order contributes to the increasing number of MH-139A helicopters under contract.
European companies are expected to report a 3.5% fall in Q4 revenue, unchanged from a week ago, and a 3.9% decline in Q4 earnings, improving from a 4.2% decrease seen last week - LSEG IBES Data.

data.title: ADS-TEC Energy delivers 10 MW power and 20 MWh storage project for Stadtwerke Mühlacker, Germany. CEO corrected to COO.
data.description: In NÜRTINGEN, Germany & MÜHLACKER, Germany, ADS-TEC Energy has successfully completed a significant battery storage project for Stadtwerke Mühlacker. The large-scale system offers 10 MW of power and 20 MWh of storage capacity. This project enhances energy system flexibility in response to increasing electricity demand. Michael Rudloff's title in the company has been corrected to COO instead of CEO. The initiative signifies a milestone in German energy development, emphasizing efficient system integration and operational procedures. ADS-TEC Energy PLC (NASDAQ: ADSE) announced the completion of this milestone project.
Genmab issues RSUs and warrants to employees.

data.title: KBRA assigns ratings to Lightpath Fiber Issuer LLC, Series 2026-1 securitization, primarily collateralized by fiber network assets.
data.description: In New York, KBRA has assigned preliminary ratings to Lightpath Fiber, LLC's Series 2026-1 (Lightpath 2026-1) communications infrastructure securitization (CIS). This issuance is backed by fiber network assets and contracts. Lightpath 2026-1 marks the inaugural securitization for Lightpath Fiber, LLC. The structure of the transaction is a master trust, enabling the potential issuance of supplementary classes.

data.title: Argentina's alternative lending market is projected to reach $2.64 billion by 2029, growing at a CAGR of 14.6% from 2020-2024.
data.description: A report titled "Argentina Alternative Lending Market Size & Forecast" has been added to ResearchAndMarkets.com's offerings. The market saw strong growth from 2020 to 2024, with a CAGR of 14.6%. The report covers over 100 KPIs including types of lending, end-user segments, loan purposes, finance models, distribution channels, and payment instruments. The market is projected to continue its growth trend in the upcoming years.

data.title: Glenn Agre secures settlement for Vector Capital Credit in Anthology Chapter 11 restructuring; resolved dispute with Anthology Inc. in a deal.
data.description: A creditor has successfully resolved a dispute with the education technology company, Anthology Inc. The law firm Glenn Agre Bergman & Fuentes LLP recently announced that their client, Vector Capital Credit, has reached a settlement in the disagreement. This news comes from New York on January 29, 2026. This resolution marks a positive development in the case, highlighting effective legal negotiations between the parties involved.

data.title: Mining sector sees record operating margins in 2026 due to brownfield advantage, with capital flowing into existing sites.
data.description: A press release from Golden Goose Resources Corp. in Vancouver, BC on January 29, 2026, reveals that the global mining sector is favoring brownfield expansion. A new study indicates that a substantial amount of capital is currently being directed towards enhancing existing mining operations. This trend signifies a strategic move within the industry to maximize the potential of established mining sites rather than investing heavily in new ventures. The shift towards brownfield expansion highlights a significant change in the investment approach of mining companies, with a focus on optimizing resources and increasing profitability through the development of existing operations.

data.title: Subsea7 gets contract for Kaikias Waterflood project in US, worth 1 sizable contract with Shell.
data.description: Subsea 7 S.A. revealed today that they have secured a significant contract from Shell for the Kaikias Waterflood project. The contract, worth a substantial amount, pertains to work on the Kaikias field located in the Mars-Ursa Basin, around 210 kilometers off the coast of Louisiana, United States.

data.title: Hotel employees face 60% higher injury risks than restaurant workers, according to OysterLink analysis.
data.description: In Miami on January 29, 2026, OysterLink, a career platform focusing on the hospitality industry, published a recent study based on federal workplace safety information. The data indicates that hotel workers are subjected to a notably greater risk of injuries compared to their peers in the restaurant field. The analysis highlights concerning disparities in safety conditions, emphasizing the need for improved safety protocols to safeguard employees.

data.title: Inventor creates SAF-T-BAG travel/tote bag, prioritizing style, versatility, practicality, and safety with innovative design.
data.description: In Pittsburgh on January 29, 2026, an inventor from Chicago, Illinois shared their vision for the SAF-T-BAG, a travel bag designed to be stylish, versatile, and practical for carrying a wide range of personal items. The inventor aimed to enhance safety by incorporating illuminating features into the bag.
